title: Initial implementation of volume plugins
body:
This implements support for mounting and unmounting volumes backed by volume plugins. Support for actually retrieving plugins requires a pull request to land in containers.conf and then that to be vendored, and as such is not yet ready. Given this, this code is only compile tested. However, the code for everything past retrieving the plugin has been written - there is support for creating, removing, mounting, and unmounting volumes, which should allow full functionality once the c/common PR is merged.

A major change is the signature of the MountPoint function for volumes, which now, by necessity, returns an error. Named volumes managed by a plugin do not have a mountpoint we control; instead, it is managed entirely by the plugin. As such, we need to cache the path in the DB, and calls to retrieve it now need to access the DB (and may fail as such).

Notably absent is support for SELinux relabelling and chowning these volumes. Given that we don't manage the mountpoint for these volumes, I am extremely reluctant to try and modify it - we could easily break the plugin trying to chown or relabel it.

Fixes #4304

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
https://github.com/containers/common/pull/371 had the c/common changes I need. From there, need a release, then I can vendor it and add support for actually retrieving plugins.

Dropping WIP. All features are now implemented.

Problem: testing. I'm going to start working towards local testing now, but getting the test environment running in CI is going to be a very interesting prospect given that it needs a separate executable/process for the plugin and containers.conf modifications.
Rebased to fix conflicts, started adding documentation to the man pages.

Made significant prgress on testing using https://github.com/mheon/testvol/ today - verified that it is possible to create and inspect volumes using the plugin. Mounting then is unfortunately hanging, but that appears to be happening in the volume plugin, not Podman, which is promising.
Great, even if we can get this partially working for next week, we can continue to fix it up after release, as long as we add no new functionality for 3.0.

Hanging issue identified as relating to copy up step - something about the way we copy files from the container into the volume *strongly* disagrees with the plugin implementation. Disabled copy-up on volumes for now, and things seem to be working otherwise. Lingering issues: does not work with SELinux (we don't chcon the mountpoint because we don't own the mountpoint; I'm still on the fence as to whether or not this is safe).

CI should now go green. There are still no tests for plugins, though; I'm working on those now.

Meanwhile, the rest of this should be ready for review. @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

I think we also need to tweak `podman cp` a bit: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/pkg/domain/infra/abi/containers_stat.go#L141

If the volume is using a driver, we may need to mount the volume and not point outside of the container's mount point.

@mheon isn't the mount point within container runtime storage for SELinux labeling?  What AVCs you are seeing?

I would figure the container volume is just following SELinux rules and should respect the :Z and :z flags,  If those flags are not specified then no SELinux should be done on the volume at all.

@vrothberg Exposing Mount/Unmount outside of Libpod would be required, and I'm very hesitant to do that in this PR - this needs to be ready and landed very soon so I can move on to other features. Sounds like a lovely bug for a future PR, though.

@rhatdan No, the mountpoint is managed by the volume plugin, not us. We do not tell them where to mount, and we do not tell them how to mount - so I'm concerned that, by changing SELinux labels, we may remove the volume plugin's access to the mountpoint. I don't think there's a dedicated SELinux type for volume plugins, so I'm honestly unsure what label they're using. Maybe the container engine one?
@rhatdan If I read https://github.com/moby/moby/pull/20834 right, Docker is not SELinux relabelling these volumes either - looks like it mostly requires SELinux to be disabled
Yup.
Tests added. Once I work out the issues and this goes green it is good to merge.
@containers/podman-maintainers This should go green now. PTAL.

@baude Seeing errors restoring the image - 
```
           github.com/containers/podman/v2/test/e2e.(*PodmanTestIntegration).RestoreArtifact(0xc0008c8fc8, 0x171f28f, 0x2c, 0x0, 0x0)
```

Mind taking a quick look?
changes LGTM @baude @rhatdan PTAL

It's green!
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL and merge

LGTM
but definitely want another head nod or three on this one.
/lgtm
